NIPA.description: National Income and Product Accounts. All tables in the NIPA catalog are fetched dynamically; each table is split into one dataset per available frequency (annual / quarterly / monthly), wide format with line items as columns.

FixedAssets.description: Fixed asset stocks, investment and depreciation. Annual only, wide format.

GDPbyIndustry.description: Value added, gross output and related series by industry. Annual and quarterly, wide format with industries as columns.

ITA.description: International Transactions Accounts. One dataset per indicator per frequency (annual, quarterly SA, quarterly NSA), long format with a single value column.
ITA.scope_notes: Only the AllCountries aggregate is ingested. The per-country / per-area breakdown is intentionally excluded — it would multiply the dataset count by ~80x with limited incremental analytical value at this stage. TSI_* pre-built time-series IDs are also filtered out (redundant with the indicator-level pulls).

Regional.description: State-level regional economic data (GDP, personal income, consumption, RPP) by line code. Long format with composite key (date, geo_fips).
Regional.scope_notes: State-level only. County, MSA and metro-level GeoFips tiers are intentionally excluded for volume reasons. Target table families are explicitly enumerated in regional_tables.TARGET_TABLES.

NIUnderlyingDetail.description: Underlying detail tables behind the NIPA aggregates. Same API shape as NIPA.

IIP.description: International Investment Position. Quarterly and annual stock data on U.S. external financial assets and liabilities.

UnderlyingGDPbyIndustry.reason: Superset of GDPbyIndustry, substantially overlapping. Deferred pending demand.

IntlServTrade.reason: International services trade. Deferred pending demand.

IntlServSTA.reason: International services supplied through affiliates. Deferred pending demand.

MNE.reason: Multinational enterprises — different data model (enterprise-level cross-sections, not time series). Deferred.

InputOutput.reason: Industry-by-industry make/use matrices; does not fit the flat time-series publishing model without a dedicated reshape. Deferred.

APIDatasetMetaData.reason: Meta endpoint only — not a data source.

freshness.strategy: Raw state tracks the last download timestamp per table/indicator with a TTL (default 7 days). After the TTL expires, the download node refetches that table. Transform nodes short-circuit via data_hash when the raw payload has not changed, so unchanged datasets are not re-merged or re-published.
freshness.stale_cutoff_behavior: Transform nodes drop datasets whose latest observation is older than (current_year - STALE_CUTOFF_YEARS). Skipped dataset IDs are logged at the end of each transform run.
